checks.evidence: src/dashboard/http_session.rs generates OS-random MCP HTTP session ids and rejects missing stateful session headers.
checks.recommendation: Keep HTTP session ids server-issued and fail closed when a stateful request omits them.

checks.evidence: src/dashboard/tool_runtime.rs builds upstream lease context from MCP/forwarded headers, metadata, and project roots.
checks.recommendation: Do not collapse HTTP clients into anonymous upstream sessions after Streamable HTTP initialization.

checks.evidence: src/dashboard.rs hashes server, client id, session id, project root, and transport when selecting an upstream pool shard.
checks.recommendation: Keep the shard selector aligned with the upstream session key fields.
Use env overrides for host-specific high-concurrency tests; keep source defaults bounded.

checks.evidence: src/client/context.rs derives stable planned leases but warns when no external session/conversation/client-instance/transport-session id exists.
checks.recommendation: For strict stdio multi-client isolation, clients should pass --session-id, MCPACE_SESSION_ID, MCPACE_CLIENT_INSTANCE_ID, or metadata.

checks.evidence: src/hub/leases.rs enforces request mutex/capacity lanes and same-session takeover rules.
checks.recommendation: Keep server policy as the source of truth for mutable resource serialization.

checks.evidence: Playwright lane uses separate BrowserContexts, parallel worker config, and recorded conflict evidence.
checks.recommendation: Run this lane on CI with at least two workers and a real browser.

checks.evidence: package.json exposes verify:multi-client-runtime and includes it in browser/experience verification.
checks.recommendation: Keep this source-only audit cheap enough to run in regular prepublish/source checks.

checks.evidence: Docs distinguish HTTP automatic sessioning from stdio/client metadata requirements and Playwright context isolation.
checks.recommendation: Do not imply MCPace can invent strict identity for clients that send no distinguishing signal.

acceptedLimits.summary: MCPace can derive a stable planned lease, but it cannot prove two same-client/same-project stdio processes are separate unless the client supplies a session/conversation/client-instance/transport-session signal.
This audit confirms source contracts and browser E2E wiring. Rust runtime parallel throughput still needs cargo build/test and live-host concurrency measurement.
